pages.raw_text:
Mayor Boyd is of the opinion that
if, two pumps were installed in the
old pumping station on the island
that the city would have plenty of
water both for fire protection and
lawn use. In speaking of the propo-
sition, he said: Il thought by sending
this message to the council a way
might be found for relieving the
threatened water famine. If the
pumping station on the island were
used the insurance men would be sat
isfed with the water condition. The
plant could be fixed up very cheaply
and lease for five or ten years could
be obtained from the Washington
Water Power company. Chief Myers
of the fire department assures me
that it would afford adequate fire pro-
lection


During the summer months enough
water could be pumped into the mains
necessary. Two pumps, I think, would
be enough. These could be purchased
for about $4,000.


1T would not be necessary to use
the pumps in the winter time except
in case of a big fire. This, in my
opinion, is the only rememdy that
would give immediate relief.


II am not in favor continued the
mayor, "of bonding proposition
either for reservoir or anything
else There is enough money going
out Of the city at present for interest
on bonds. The city is enough in
debt" p.
